claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 19 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 19 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're asking about.

Let me start with: **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you're inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child I'm responsible for. I'm inquiring about one child - my son Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'll help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for your son Alex.

Let me ask you the first question: **How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 19 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 19 years old, I need to ask some additional questions.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

(This would include things like A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs up to level 3, or certain other approved courses.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. Alex is studying for his A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful to know. Since Alex is 19 and in approved education, I need to check a few more details.

**Did Alex start or restart his A-level course before he turned 19?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he did start before he turned 19.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Now I need to ask:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to understand your residency status:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. I have a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Are you the only person responsible for Alex, or is there someone else (such as another parent or partner) who also cares for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm the primary person responsible for him. He lives with me full-time.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to ask a couple more questions:

**Is Alex currently in hospital or in local authority care?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in hospital or in care. He's at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just one more important question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me carefully review Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit based on what you've told me.

**Based on the information you've provided:**

- Alex is 19 years old
- He is studying for his A-levels (approved education)
- He started this course before turning 19
- He lives with you in the UK (Manchester)
- He is not in hospital or care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- You are responsible for him

**The key consideration here is Alex's age and education status.** 

According to the Child Benefit rules, you can continue to claim Child Benefit for a young person aged 16-19 if they are in approved education or training. However, Child Benefit for young people in education or training **stops on 31 August after their 19th birthday**, regardless of whether they are still in education.

**Critical question:** Has Alex already passed his 19th birthday, and if so, **when did he turn 19?** Specifically, has the 31 August following his 19th birthday already passed?

This timing is crucial to determining current eligibility.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, I see what you're asking. Yes, Alex has already turned 19 - that was several months ago, back in the spring. So yes, the 31st of August after his birthday has already passed.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Since Alex turned 19 in the spring and the 31st of August following his 19th birthday has now passed, I have enough information to make a determination.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

**Unfortunately, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

**Reasoning:** While Alex meets many of the criteria - he lives with you, is in approved education (A-levels), and started that education before turning 19 - there is a specific age limit that applies. Child Benefit for young people aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training stops on **31 August following their 19th birthday**, regardless of whether they continue in education after that date.

Since Alex turned 19 in the spring and the 31st of August has already passed, he has now exceeded the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, even though he is still studying for his A-levels.

If you were previously receiving Child Benefit for Alex, it should have stopped on 31 August. If you're still receiving payments, you should contact HMRC to let them know, as continuing to receive payments after eligibility ends could result in overpayments that need to be repaid.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?
